meaning: <意第绪>n.(尤指第二次世界大战前东欧的)犹太人小村(或小镇)
examples: The warmth of the shtetl feeds the creativity of these nascent cinephiles.
Or a Tevye power-lunging out of the shtetl, schlepping an elliptical machine in his cart.
Born in 1932 in a Lithuanian shtetl, Mr. Kagan was raised in Brownsville, which was then a working-class Jewish neighborhood.
Smela, two hours' drive south of Korsun-Shevchenkovsky, used to be a shtetl.
